title: lathyrus nissolia subsp. amanus
intro: Lathyrus nissolia subsp. amanus, also known as the bitter pea or Italian vetch, is a species of flowering plant in the legume family, characterized by its purple or white flowers and edible seeds. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been reported to help alleviate various health issues. The seeds of Lathyrus nissolia subsp. amanus have been used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, and are also valued for their potential health benefits in reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Lathyrus nissolia subsp. amanus can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies in the form of teas, capsules, or powders.
